prompt:
Tech-debt ticket, and I finally got it prioritized: orderdesk.py has become the file every order-touching change collides in. Pricing, tax, shipping and the printed report all live in one module, three teams edit it, and last sprint two merge conflicts in a row were people stepping on each other in unrelated sections. I want it split along the seams that are already obviously there.

Target layout: an orderdesk PACKAGE — orderdesk/pricing.py (quantity breaks, line totals, subtotal), orderdesk/tax.py (rate table, tax_cents), orderdesk/shipping.py (weight bands, free-shipping threshold, total_weight_g, shipping_cents), orderdesk/report.py (render_report), and orderdesk/__init__.py as a facade so every existing caller keeps working: import orderdesk; orderdesk.order_total(o), orderdesk.render_report(o), orderdesk.price_lines(o) etc. must all still resolve, and the facade names must be the same function objects as the ones in the domain modules, not copies or wrappers. The rate tables (PRICE_BREAKS, TAX_RATES_BP, SHIPPING_BANDS, FREE_SHIPPING_MIN_CENTS) move with their modules — the ops notebook imports those directly. When you're done the old single-file orderdesk.py must be gone; the package replaces it.

This is a pure reshuffle: observable behavior must not change AT ALL. Same integer-cents arithmetic, same per-line tax flooring, same band boundaries, and render_report stays byte-for-byte identical — fulfillment prints and files these. test_orderdesk.py is the acceptance contract (python3 test_orderdesk.py); it pins the new layout plus the numbers the old module produced. Don't touch the test file.
